mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal income indicates corporate profit margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1 and RSAFS movements show no predictive power for 60-day returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects expanding corporate output and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery periods (out-of-sample accuracy < 52%).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly CPI inflation above 0.4% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity discount rates and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal strengthening consumer confidence and construction sector demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST changes show zero correlation with 5-day forward returns across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 60-day returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% indicates severe supply-side contraction and falling business investment, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of quarters where INDPRO drops > 1.0% QoQ.
